Episteme 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.episteme.social.economics
  2. Resource

Uses of Class
org.episteme.social.economics.Resource

Packages that use Resource
Package
Description
org.episteme.social.architecture
Provides mathematical and analytical models for architectural engineering, building physics, and urban history.
org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic
Provides models and simulation engines for urban traffic flow analysis.
org.episteme.social.arts
- artworks, music, literature classification.
org.episteme.social.arts.music
- musical notes, instruments, compositions.
org.episteme.social.arts.theater
- plays, performances, dramatic arts.
org.episteme.social.economics
Provides comprehensive mathematical models for economic systems, agent-based modeling, and financial analysis.
org.episteme.social.economics.resources
Provides classes and interfaces for representing economic resources.
org.episteme.social.history.archeology
Provides classes and interfaces for archaeological research and analysis.

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Building
    Represents a building or architectural structure, integrating historical, geographical, and stylistic dimensions.

    Class
    Description
    class 
    Vehicle
    Represents a generic vehicle entity within a macro or microscopic traffic system.

  • Uses of Resource in org.episteme.social.arts

    Subclasses of Resource in org.episteme.social.arts
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Artwork
    Represents a piece of art (Artwork), integrating historical, geographical, economic, and scientific dimensions.
    class 
    Book
    Represents a book or publication within the literary arts.
    class 
    Film
    Represents a film or motion picture within the cinematic arts.
    class 
    Picture
    Represents a visual artwork such as a painting, drawing, or photograph.

    Composition
    Represents a musical composition with multiple tracks and signatures.

    Subclasses of Resource in org.episteme.social.economics
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    HumanResource
    Models human labor and expertise as an economic resource. * @version 1.1
    class 
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Resource
    EarthEcosource.generateResource(String name, String description, Quantity<?> amount)
    Generates a new resource from the Earth.
    Methods in org.episteme.social.economics that return types with arguments of type Resource
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Set<Resource>
    Trade.getAgent1Resources()
    Returns an unmodifiable view of resources from agent 1.
    Set<Resource>
    Trade.getAgent2Resources()
    Returns an unmodifiable view of resources from agent 2.
    Set<Resource>
    EconomicAgent.getBelongings()
    Returns the set of resources currently owned by this agent.
    Set<Resource>
    Factory.getInventory()
    Returns the set of resources currently held by the factory.
    Set<Resource>
    Task.getProducts()
     
    Set<Resource>
    Community.getResources()
     
    Set<Resource>
    Organization.getResources()
     
    Set<Resource>
    Task.getResources()
     
    Set<Resource>
    TaskProcessor.getResources()
    Returns the set of resources available to this processor.
    Set<Resource>
    Barter.getResources1()

    Barter.getResources2()
     
    Methods in org.episteme.social.economics with parameters of type Resource
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    EconomicAgent.addBelonging(Resource belonging)
    Transfers ownership of a resource to this agent.
    void
    Task.addProduct(Resource product)
     
    void
    Community.addResource(Resource resource)
     
    void
    Task.addResource(Resource resource)
     
    Quantity<?>
    Economy.getNumberOfUnits(Resource resource)
    Aggregates the total quantity of a specific resource across all organizations in the current economy.
    void
    EconomicAgent.removeBelonging(Resource belonging)
    Relinquishes ownership of a resource.
    void
    Community.removeResource(Resource resource)

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Performs a direct resource swap with another community.

    Sets the resources owned by this agent.
    void
    Community.setResources(Set<Resource> resources)

    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    Barter(EconomicAgent agent1, Set<Resource> resources1, EconomicAgent agent2, Set<Resource> resources2)
    Initializes a barter contract.

  • Uses of Resource in org.episteme.social.economics.resources

    Subclasses of Resource in org.episteme.social.economics.resources
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Artifact
    Represents a resource created by human craftsmanship (an artifact).
    class 
    Building
    Represents a human-made physical structure such as a house, factory, or skyscraper. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Clothes
    Represents wearable items. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Creature
    Represents a living biological resource (animal, plant, etc.). * @version 1.1
    class 
    Fossil
    Represents fossilized remains of ancient organisms. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Gas
    Represents natural resources in gaseous form. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Installation
    Represents a large-scale structure or artwork installed in a specific location.
    class 
    Liquid
    Represents natural resources in liquid form. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Machine
    Represents a complex tool (machine) that consumes energy to perform work.
    class 
    Mineral
    A class representing Minerals.
    class 
    Natural
    Represents resources found naturally in the environment (planets, stars, living organisms, minerals). * @version 1.1
    class 
    PhysicalObject
    Represents a crafted object that can be moved and used (e.g., tools, appliances, small structures).
    class 
    Road
    Represents a constructed path or road infrastructure.
    class 
    Robot
    Represents an autonomous machine (Robot).
    class 
    Solid
    Represents natural resources in solid form (e.g., stones, rocks). * @version 1.1
    class 
    Thing
    Represents a tangible physical object that occupies space and may have a 3D representation.
    class 
    Tool
    Represents a tool used to build or repair other objects.
    class 
    Vehicle
    Represents a machine capable of transporting entities.

    Method

    Item
